days.title: JUnit 5 & Mockito
learningObjectives: Explain JUnit 5 & Mockito with phase-10 depth and clarity
Implement Java examples for JUnit 5 & Mockito with production awareness
Identify failure risks and mitigation strategies
Reason about trade-offs under realistic constraints
Design or optimize solutions with maintainability in mind
Present interview answers with concrete evidence and structure

sections.title: Why JUnit 5 & Mockito matters
sections.content:
JUnit 5 & Mockito is a high-value Day 85 topic in Phase 10 (testing, performance diagnostics, and interview readiness).

At this stage, interviewers evaluate whether you can connect implementation details with reliability, scalability, and maintainability outcomes.

What strong answers include:
1. precise concept framing,
2. operational implications,
3. one realistic failure pattern,
4. one practical mitigation.

This topic is not just about code that compiles. It is about decisions that survive production traffic and team ownership.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - JUnit 5 & Mockito
sections.content:
### Day 85: JUnit 5 & Mockito
Phase context: testing, performance diagnostics, and interview readiness.

### What to understand deeply
- mechanism and boundaries
- runtime behavior and cost
- operational failure modes
- trade-offs vs alternatives

### Implementation workflow
1. Build a clear baseline solution.
2. Validate edge/failure paths.
3. Measure impact (latency, memory, throughput, or complexity).
4. Refactor for readability and maintainability.

### Interview framing template
- "The problem this solves is..."
- "I chose this approach because..."
- "The key trade-off is..."
- "A failure case is... and mitigation is..."

### Production perspective
For JUnit 5 & Mockito, quality is judged by correctness + resilience + debuggability, not just API usage.

sections.title: Basic Walkthrough
sections.description: JUnit-style @Test: assert expected vs actual (plain assertions).

sections.title: Intermediate Implementation
sections.description: Test double stub: returns canned values for collaborators.

sections.title: Advanced Production Pattern
sections.description: Mock: verify interaction — method called with expected argument.

sections.title: Common Pitfalls
items: Over-mocking tests and missing integration confidence.
Skipping performance baselines before optimization.
Analyzing dumps/logs without hypothesis and scope.
Practicing interview questions without structured debrief.
Focusing on syntax over communication and reasoning quality.

sections.title: Exercise - JUnit 5 & Mockito Applied Scenario
sections.problem:
Implement a realistic solution for **JUnit 5 & Mockito**.

Requirements:
1. Define assumptions and constraints first.
2. Include one failure-path handling strategy.
3. Provide one improvement over baseline.
4. State complexity or runtime impact.
5. Keep implementation review-friendly.
hints: Add one observability/debug aid in output or comments.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is JUnit 5 architecture?
conceptual.answer: Jupiter platform vintage—annotations @Test @BeforeEach extensions model.

conceptual.question: What is Mockito?
conceptual.answer: Mocking framework stubbing verify—@Mock @InjectMocks lenient strictness.

conceptual.question: What is test pyramid?
conceptual.answer: Many unit fewer integration fewer e2e—cost speed reliability balance.

conceptual.question: What is AAA pattern?
conceptual.answer: Arrange Act Assert—readable tests structure.

conceptual.question: What is stub vs mock?
conceptual.answer: Stub returns canned data; mock asserts interactions occurred—terminology often blurred Mockito.

conceptual.question: What is parameterized tests?
conceptual.answer: @ParameterizedTest multiple inputs edge cases data driven.

conceptual.question: What is test doubles taxonomy?
conceptual.answer: Fake dummy stub spy mock—Gerard Meszaros precise language sometimes overkill.

conceptual.question: What is AssertJ fluent assertions?
conceptual.answer: Readable assertions chaining soft assertions multiple failures.

conceptual.question: What is Mockito verify times never
conceptual.answer: Interaction verification—avoid overspecified brittle tests.

conceptual.question: What is static mocking Mockito inline?
conceptual.answer: Mockito 3+ inline mockStatic—use sparingly design for testability better.

conceptual.question: What is Spring test slices?
conceptual.answer: @WebMvcTest @DataJpaTest focused context faster.

conceptual.question: What is flaky tests?
conceptual.answer: Non-deterministic timing order—quarantine fix root cause not ignore.

conceptual.question: What is code coverage metric?
conceptual.answer: Line branch coverage—tool not goal missing assertions meaningless.

conceptual.question: What is mutation testing PIT?
conceptual.answer: Tests quality kill mutants—stronger than coverage alone expensive.

conceptual.question: What is TDD red green refactor?
conceptual.answer: Discipline write failing test minimal code refactor—benefits debate pragmatic.

codeBased.question: Mockito.lenient stubbing
codeBased.answer: Avoid unnecessary stubbing exception strict stubs.

seniorScenario.question: CI flaky tests ignored culture?
seniorScenario.answer: Dangerous—quarantine track fix ownership—quality culture.

seniorScenario.question: Mocking domain behavior too much?
seniorScenario.answer: Tests pass but production fails—prefer real domain unit pure.

seniorScenario.question: Coverage 90% still bugs?
seniorScenario.answer: Assertions weak mutation testing reviews.

seniorScenario.question: Testing private methods directly?
seniorScenario.answer: Test via public behavior refactor extract class if needed.

seniorScenario.question: Parallel test execution Spring context cache?
seniorScenario.answer: Reuse contexts @DirtiesContext expensive misuse.

seniorScenario.question: Contract testing vs unit tests investment?
seniorScenario.answer: Balance pyramid consumer-driven Pact ROI.

wrongAnswers: JUnit 4 and JUnit 5 use the exact same package names.
Mockito can mock final classes and static methods in all versions without setup.
100% code coverage guarantees correctness.
verify() should be used on every mock in every test.
@SpringBootTest is the best choice for every unit test.
Flaky tests should be retried indefinitely in CI without investigation.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Area | Phase 10 Expectation | Quick Rule | Gotcha |
|---|---|---|---|
| Framing | Explain intent + constraints | Start from problem | API-only answer |
| Design | State trade-off explicitly | Compare alternatives | One-solution bias |
| Runtime | Mention cost impact | Time + space/ops | No performance reasoning |
| Failure | Name one incident pattern | Mitigation path | Happy-path only thinking |
| Communication | Be concise + concrete | Use one example | Generic buzzwords |

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is @SpringBootTest?
conceptual.answer: Full application context integration test—slower broad scope.

conceptual.question: What is @WebMvcTest?
conceptual.answer: Slice only web layer MockMvc controllers—mock beans fast.

conceptual.question: What is @DataJpaTest?
conceptual.answer: JPA slice embedded database default—repository tests.

conceptual.question: What is Testcontainers?
conceptual.answer: Docker containers for real DB Kafka etc.—integration fidelity—CI requirements Docker.

conceptual.question: What is @DynamicPropertySource Testcontainers?
conceptual.answer: Inject random ports JDBC URL into Spring context dynamic.

conceptual.question: What is @MockBean vs @MockitoBean?
conceptual.answer: Spring Boot 3.4+ MockitoBean refined—mock injection clarity evolving.

conceptual.question: What is slice vs full context trade-off?
conceptual.answer: Speed vs confidence—choose appropriate layer.

conceptual.question: What is WireMock?
conceptual.answer: Stub external HTTP services—deterministic tests no real third party.

conceptual.question: What is Awaitility async tests?
conceptual.answer: Poll until condition timeout—reactive async flows.

conceptual.question: What is Spring Security test @WithMockUser?
conceptual.answer: Security context in tests—authorize requests.

conceptual.question: What is @Sql scripts test database?
conceptual.answer: Seed data before test—cleanup scripts.

conceptual.question: What is TestRestTemplate vs WebTestClient?
conceptual.answer: Servlet vs reactive testing stacks.

conceptual.question: What is @Nested JUnit 5?
conceptual.answer: Organize test classes hierarchical structure readability.

conceptual.question: What is ArchUnit architecture tests?
conceptual.answer: Enforce package dependencies cycles—CI guardrails.

conceptual.question: What is testcontainers reuse Ryuk?
conceptual.answer: Resource reaper container—speed local dev mode experimental.

seniorScenario.question: Testcontainers CI slow flaky docker?
seniorScenario.answer: Ryuk permissions daemon rootless docker—stabilize runners.

seniorScenario.question: Shared test database pollution?
seniorScenario.answer: Isolation per test class transactions random schema Testcontainers fresh.

seniorScenario.question: Full Spring context every unit test suite 30 min?
seniorScenario.answer: Refactor slices architecture faster feedback loops.

seniorScenario.question: Production-like data volume tests?
seniorScenario.answer: Subset generation anonymized GDPR compliant performance tests separate env.

seniorScenario.question: Contract drift consumer provider?
seniorScenario.answer: Spring Cloud Contract Pact pipeline gates releases.

seniorScenario.question: Flaky docker port collisions parallel CI?
seniorScenario.answer: Random ports dynamic properties serialization race conditions rare.

wrongAnswers: @DataJpaTest loads the entire Spring Boot application always.
Testcontainers runs containers in-memory without Docker.
MockMvc requires a running servlet container always.
@SpringBootTest is faster than @WebMvcTest.
WireMock can only stub HTTPS with a valid public certificate always.
Integration tests should never use transactions or rollbacks.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is consumer-driven contract testing?
conceptual.answer: Consumer defines expected interaction provider verifies—Pact popular.

conceptual.question: What is Pact broker?
conceptual.answer: Contract repository versioning deployment coordination—can I deploy matrix.

conceptual.question: What is provider verification?
conceptual.answer: Replay requests from contract against provider—fail if breaks consumers.

conceptual.question: What is schema registry compatibility CI?
conceptual.answer: Avro breaking change detection automated—similar governance.

conceptual.question: What is Spring Cloud Contract?
conceptual.answer: Groovy DSL wiremock generation producer tests—alternative to Pact.

conceptual.question: What is end-to-end test drawbacks?
conceptual.answer: Slow flaky expensive—few high value journeys not everything.

conceptual.question: What is synthetic monitoring canary?
conceptual.answer: Prod traffic simulation monitoring SLIs proactive—different from CI tests.

conceptual.question: What is chaos testing in testing strategy?
conceptual.answer: Different from unit—validate resilience assumptions complement not replace.

conceptual.question: What is mutation testing in CI cost?
conceptual.answer: CPU time expensive selective critical modules periodic nightly maybe.

conceptual.question: What is test data management?
conceptual.answer: Anonymized realistic GDPR fixtures factories—maintainability investment.

conceptual.question: What is parallel CI test execution?
conceptual.answer: JUnit parallel surefire forks—test isolation thread safety static state bugs.

conceptual.question: What is quality gates SonarQube?
conceptual.answer: Coverage thresholds duplication smells—enforced thoughtfully avoid gaming.

conceptual.question: What is shift-left security testing?
conceptual.answer: SAST DAST dependencies early pipeline DevSecOps culture.

conceptual.question: What is performance testing types?
conceptual.answer: Load stress spike soak—different goals tools k6 JMeter Gatling.

conceptual.question: What is accessibility testing?
conceptual.answer: Automated axe checks manual screen reader—quality inclusive product.

seniorScenario.question: Pact broker downtime blocks deploy?
seniorScenario.answer: Cache artifacts fallback process resilience business continuity.

seniorScenario.question: Contracts green prod broken?
seniorScenario.answer: Environment differences auth data contracts incomplete coverage gaps.

seniorScenario.question: E2E suite 6 hours nightly only feedback slow?
seniorScenario.answer: Shift tests earlier pyramid balance investment automation ROI.

seniorScenario.question: Security scan false positives developer fatigue?
seniorScenario.answer: Triage workflow suppress with justification not blanket ignore.

seniorScenario.question: Performance tests non-representative data?
seniorScenario.answer: Production-like shape masking privacy sampling techniques.

seniorScenario.question: Accessibility last minute audit fail release?
seniorScenario.answer: Continuous a11y linting design system shift left culture.

wrongAnswers: Contract testing replaces the need for any integration tests.
Pact guarantees your microservices will never fail in production.
E2E tests should cover every possible user click path.
Mutation testing is fast and should run on every commit always.
Schema registry compatibility checks replace the need for code review.
Synthetic monitoring replaces real user monitoring entirely.

4. State complexity or runtime impact.
5. Keep implementation review-friendly.
hints: Add one observability/debug aid in output or comments.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is JVM profiling?
conceptual.answer: Analyze CPU allocation locks threads—find hotspots bottlenecks—not guessing.

conceptual.question: What is JFR Java Flight Recorder?
conceptual.answer: Low overhead profiling events—JDK Mission Control analysis—production safe often.

conceptual.question: What is async-profiler?
conceptual.answer: CPU flame graphs wall clock modes—combined with JFR powerful.

conceptual.question: What is heap dump analysis?
conceptual.answer: MAT Eclipse find dominator tree memory leaks retained objects—OOME debugging.

conceptual.question: What is GC log analysis?
conceptual.answer: Pause times allocation rate promotion failures tuning collectors G1 ZGC.

conceptual.question: What is JIT compilation profiling?
conceptual.answer: Tiered compilation deoptimization uncommon—understand warmup benchmarks.

conceptual.question: What is lock contention profiling?
conceptual.answer: JFR thread states parking monitors—scale threads or reduce locking.

conceptual.question: What is allocation profiling?
conceptual.answer: TLAB allocations object churn—reduce allocations hot loops.

conceptual.question: What is native memory tracking?
conceptual.answer: Direct ByteBuffers JNI metaspace thread stacks—off-heap leaks.

conceptual.question: What is continuous profiling production?
conceptual.answer: Datadog Continuous Profiler Pyroscope—always-on overhead budget.

conceptual.question: What is benchmarking pitfalls?
conceptual.answer: JMH correct warmup forks blackholes dead code elimination—scientific rigor.

conceptual.question: What is flame graph interpretation?
conceptual.answer: Width time cumulative—plateau hotspots focus optimization ROI.

conceptual.question: What is safepoint bias profiling?
conceptual.answer: Some profilers distort—be aware async profiler mitigations.

conceptual.question: What is JDWP remote debugging overhead?
conceptual.answer: Never leave enabled production—massive performance security risks.

conceptual.question: What is Arthas Alibaba diagnostic?
conceptual.answer: Dynamic tracing attach production advanced—power danger.

codeBased.question: jmap -dump:live,format=b,file=heap.hprof
codeBased.answer: Heap dump command careful prod impact.

seniorScenario.question: Production CPU spike profiling authorization?
seniorScenario.answer: Legal privacy sampling consent incident response process.

seniorScenario.question: Profiling shows GC not issue CPU app—still tune GC?
seniorScenario.answer: Focus app algorithm first measurement-driven.

seniorScenario.question: Heap dump contains PII GDPR?
seniorScenario.answer: Secure handling encrypted storage limited access delete after resolution.

seniorScenario.question: Benchmark shows fast but prod slow?
seniorScenario.answer: Different data JIT warmup infrastructure noise—reproduce prod-like.

seniorScenario.question: Continuous profiler overhead unacceptable tier?
seniorScenario.answer: Sampling frequency trade-offs budget alerts.

seniorScenario.question: Arthas in prod regulatory bank?
seniorScenario.answer: Change management risk approval audit trails strong controls.

wrongAnswers: Profiling always has zero overhead.
Heap dumps should be taken on every request in production.
JFR is only for development laptops.
More heap always solves OutOfMemoryError.
Microbenchmarks in main() without JMH are always valid.
GC logs are irrelevant if average pause is low.

sections.title: Common Pitfalls
items: Over-mocking tests and missing integration confidence.
Skipping performance baselines before optimization.
Analyzing dumps/logs without hypothesis and scope.
Practicing interview questions without structured debrief.
Focusing on syntax over communication and reasoning quality.

sections.title: Exercise - System Design Case Study + Behavioral Applied Scenario
sections.problem:
Implement a realistic solution for **System Design Case Study + Behavioral**.

Requirements:
1. Define assumptions and constraints first.
2. Include one failure-path handling strategy.
3. Provide one improvement over baseline.
4. State complexity or runtime impact.
5. Keep implementation review-friendly.
hints: Add one observability/debug aid in output or comments.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is STAR method?
conceptual.answer: Situation Task Action Result—behavioral interview structure metrics concise.

conceptual.question: What is system design interview flow?
conceptual.answer: Clarify requirements estimate API data model deep dive scale bottlenecks trade-offs.

conceptual.question: What is trade-off communication?
conceptual.answer: Explicit CAP latency consistency cost—interviewers want reasoning not buzzwords only.

conceptual.question: What is estimation sanity?
conceptual.answer: Round numbers orders of magnitude—show structured thinking assumptions stated.

conceptual.question: What is clarifying questions importance?
conceptual.answer: Avoid solving wrong problem—requirements ambiguous intentionally sometimes.

conceptual.question: What is drawing diagrams?
conceptual.answer: Sequence diagrams capacity sketches—whiteboard communication clarity.

conceptual.question: What is behavioral + system combo rounds?
conceptual.answer: Companies mix—Amazon LP + system design—prepare both.

conceptual.question: What is coding vs system design seniority?
conceptual.answer: More design less Leetcode sometimes—not universal—research company.

conceptual.question: What is “tell me about a failure” question?
conceptual.answer: Honesty learning accountability—no blaming others excessively.

conceptual.question: What is conflict resolution story?
conceptual.answer: Collaboration empathy outcome—team dynamics signal.

conceptual.question: What is leadership without authority?
conceptual.answer: Influence persuasion data-driven decisions cross-team initiatives.

conceptual.question: What is diversity inclusion question?
conceptual.answer: Respect inclusive language policies—authenticity not rehearsed platitudes only.

conceptual.question: What is salary negotiation timing?
conceptual.answer: After offer in hand—ranges research levels.fyi tactfully—varies region culture.

conceptual.question: What is mock interview value?
conceptual.answer: Feedback loop reduce anxiety time management practice—peers platforms.

conceptual.question: What is continuous learning narrative?
conceptual.answer: Side projects certifications open source—growth mindset evidence.

codeBased.question: Draw DB schema primary foreign keys
Hash partition vs range partition trade-off
Write pseudocode rate limiter token bucket
CAP choice for bank vs social feed
SLA calculation downtime minutes per year

seniorScenario.question: Tell me production outage you led—structure?
seniorScenario.answer: Detection mitigation root cause prevention metrics communication.

seniorScenario.question: Disagreed with manager technical decision?
seniorScenario.answer: Data respect escalation outcomes—maturity signal.

seniorScenario.question: Scaled team hiring mentoring?
seniorScenario.answer: Onboarding quality bar diversity interviewing experience.

seniorScenario.question: Inherited legacy system strategy?
seniorScenario.answer: Strangler incremental risk management stakeholder communication.

seniorScenario.question: Security incident communication?
seniorScenario.answer: Transparency legal obligations customer trust timelines.

seniorScenario.question: Prioritization tech debt vs features?
seniorScenario.answer: Framework quantifying risk product partnership error budgets.

wrongAnswers: You should never ask clarifying questions in a system design interview.
STAR stories should be at least 20 minutes long.
The only goal of behavioral interviews is to test memorized answers.
System design interviews never require any math.
You should criticize your previous employer to show honesty.
Negotiation should happen before you know the role scope.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Area | Phase 10 Expectation | Quick Rule | Gotcha |
|---|---|---|---|
| Framing | Explain intent + constraints | Start from problem | API-only answer |
| Design | State trade-off explicitly | Compare alternatives | One-solution bias |
| Runtime | Mention cost impact | Time + space/ops | No performance reasoning |
| Failure | Name one incident pattern | Mitigation path | Happy-path only thinking |
| Communication | Be concise + concrete | Use one example | Generic buzzwords |

days.title: Full Mock Interview Simulation
learningObjectives: Explain Full Mock Interview Simulation with phase-10 depth and clarity
Implement Java examples for Full Mock Interview Simulation with production awareness
Identify failure risks and mitigation strategies
Reason about trade-offs under realistic constraints
Design or optimize solutions with maintainability in mind
Present interview answers with concrete evidence and structure

sections.title: Why Full Mock Interview Simulation matters
sections.content:
Full Mock Interview Simulation is a high-value Day 90 topic in Phase 10 (testing, performance diagnostics, and interview readiness).

At this stage, interviewers evaluate whether you can connect implementation details with reliability, scalability, and maintainability outcomes.

What strong answers include:
1. precise concept framing,
2. operational implications,
3. one realistic failure pattern,
4. one practical mitigation.

This topic is not just about code that compiles. It is about decisions that survive production traffic and team ownership.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Full Mock Interview Simulation
sections.content:
### Day 90: Full Mock Interview Simulation
Phase context: testing, performance diagnostics, and interview readiness.

### What to understand deeply
- mechanism and boundaries
- runtime behavior and cost
- operational failure modes
- trade-offs vs alternatives

### Implementation workflow
1. Build a clear baseline solution.
2. Validate edge/failure paths.
3. Measure impact (latency, memory, throughput, or complexity).
4. Refactor for readability and maintainability.

### Interview framing template
- "The problem this solves is..."
- "I chose this approach because..."
- "The key trade-off is..."
- "A failure case is... and mitigation is..."

### Production perspective
For Full Mock Interview Simulation, quality is judged by correctness + resilience + debuggability, not just API usage.

sections.title: Basic Walkthrough
sections.description: Timed answer structure: clarify → approach → complexity → code sketch.

sections.title: Intermediate Implementation
sections.output: I would test empty input and overflow
sections.description: Think-aloud while coding: narrate invariants and tests you would add.

sections.title: Advanced Production Pattern
sections.description: Self-review checklist: correctness, complexity, edge cases, production ops.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is full mock interview simulation?
conceptual.answer: Timed multi-section coding system design behavioral—mirror real process reduce anxiety.

conceptual.question: What is interviewer roleplay feedback?
conceptual.answer: External coach peer—blind spots communication clarity time management.

conceptual.question: What is rubric self-assessment?
conceptual.answer: Grade communication correctness trade-offs depth—structured improvement plan.

conceptual.question: What is time boxing practice?
conceptual.answer: 45 min problem—force prioritization MVP partial solutions better than incomplete perfectionism paralysis.

conceptual.question: What is articulating thought process?
conceptual.answer: Think aloud—interviewers evaluate reasoning not only final answer silent genius fails communication.

conceptual.question: What is managing stress physiology?
conceptual.answer: Sleep breathing breaks growth mindset—performance psychology underrated.

conceptual.question: What is company-specific research?
conceptual.answer: Engineering blog values interview loop—tailor examples stories—authenticity.

conceptual.question: What is follow-up thank you note?
conceptual.answer: Some cultures companies matter—brief professional gratitude not desperate.

conceptual.question: What is offer evaluation beyond salary?
conceptual.answer: Equity benefits growth manager team tech stack remote flexibility holistic life decision.

conceptual.question: What is continuous iteration?
conceptual.answer: Each mock interview one improvement focus communication speed edge cases testing habit.

conceptual.question: What is portfolio GitHub polish?
conceptual.answer: Readme clarity runnable demos—recruiters skim quickly first impression.

conceptual.question: What is reference preparation?
conceptual.answer: Align stories with references former managers—consistent narrative ethical honesty.

conceptual.question: What is negotiation multiple offers?
conceptual.answer: Ethical transparency timelines respectful—burning bridges costly small industry.

conceptual.question: What is onboarding plan after offer?
conceptual.answer: Learning roadmap first 30 60 90—hit ground running reputation early.

conceptual.question: What is lifelong interview skill?
conceptual.answer: Even as hiring manager interviewing candidates—symmetry empathy calibration.

codeBased.question: Checklist before interview sleep food materials
Flashcards system design concepts spaced repetition
Write stories STAR file spreadsheet
codeBased.answer: Organize examples multiple prompts reuse adapt.

codeBased.question: Company values map stories Amazon LP
System design template outline personal
codeBased.answer: Headings reuse accelerate structure under pressure.

seniorScenario.question: Mock interview feedback brutal—emotion?
seniorScenario.answer: Growth mindset separate self-worth from performance iteration professional athlete mindset.

seniorScenario.question: Imposter syndrome strong?
seniorScenario.answer: Common high performers—evidence journal achievements objective reality therapy resources.

seniorScenario.question: Burnout during job search?
seniorScenario.answer: Sustainable schedule boundaries support network health priority long career.

seniorScenario.question: Multiple rejections demoralizing?
seniorScenario.answer: Stochastic process variance—learn per round persistence calibrated improvement not luck only.

seniorScenario.question: Onsite travel logistics remote hybrid?
seniorScenario.answer: Adapt preparation environment camera lighting internet backup plan.

seniorScenario.question: Post-offer negotiation fear conflict?
seniorScenario.answer: Framing collaboration mutual fit preparation scripts professional respect—practice roleplay.

wrongAnswers: Mock interviews are useless compared to only reading books.
You should memorize one story that answers every behavioral question.
Full mock simulations guarantee you will pass real interviews.
Communication skills do not matter if your code is perfect.
You should accept the first offer immediately without questions.
Preparation ends when you get the job offer.

|---|---|---|---|
| Framing | Explain intent + constraints | Start from problem | API-only answer |
